free_text: Low energy scattering cross section ratios of
Ruggedized silicon photodiode detectors were
used and mounted at Theta(lab) = 30, 90, 105, 135,
150, and 165 deg, approximately 6 cm from the center
of the gas target.
Statistical uncertainty
Compiled by S.H.
Experiment performed using 4 MV KN Van
de Graaff accelerator at the University of Notre
Dame's Nuclear Science Laboratory
Windowless gas-target system RHINOCEROS was used.
Nitrogen gas, depleted in 15N and kept at a pressure
of approx. 0.25 Torr, was used throughout the
experiment, which corresponds to a target thickness
of approx. 0.5 mu-g/cm2
Data presented in fig. 4 of the reference
sent by author (R.deB.)
Experiment performed using 10 MV FN
Tandem Van de Graaff accelerator at the University of
Notre Dame's Nuclear Science Laboratory.
Data presented in fig. 7 of the reference
sent by author (R.deB.)
Target was an 20 mu-g/cm2 thick layer of Adenine
(C5H5N5) of natural isotopic abundance evaporated on
a carbon backing foil, also of natural abunda
of similar thickness.
